title: chaetogastra kingii
intro: Chaetogastra kingii, also known as the "King's Crown" or "Rat's Head Moss," is a type of lichenized fungus that belongs to the family Gomphillaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Chaetogastra kingii include its potential to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Chaetogastra kingii is typically prepared as a tincture or decoction, and can also be used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
